pattern: A one-dimensional cut array, filled with help from a precomputed palindrome table, gives the fewest cuts by extending the best prefix solution at each position.
hints: The answer is one less than the smallest number of palindromic pieces the string can be split into.
Precompute which substrings are palindromes, then let cuts[i] be the minimum cuts for the prefix of length i+1, updating it from every j where the tail s[j..i] is a palindrome.
Build a boolean table pal[j][i] for s[j..i] being a palindrome; set cuts[i] = 0 if pal[0][i], else min over j<=i of cuts[j-1]+1 when pal[j][i]; return cuts[n-1].

edge_inputs.description: one cut splits into two palindromes
two distinct characters need one cut
whole string already a palindrome, zero cuts
all distinct characters force a cut between each

stress.description: random small-alphabet strings so palindromic substrings appear often
stress.note: three-letter alphabet yields frequent palindromic runs
